heading: Unauthorized use of pre-1972 sound recordings
label: 1401 Unauthorized use of pre-1972 sound recordings
notes: References in TextThe date of enactment of this section, referred to in subsecs. (a)(2)(B)(i), (c)(3), (5)(A), (d)(2)–(4)(A), (e)(1), (f)(1)(B), (5)(A)(ii), (B)(i), (ii), and ()(2), is the date of enactment of , which was approved .lPub. L. 115–264Oct. 11, 2018The year in which this section is enacted, referred to in subsec. (d)(2)(B), is the year in which was enacted. Such Act was approved .Pub. L. 115–264Oct. 11, 2018

children.heading: .—Unauthorized acts
children.label: 1 .—Unauthorized acts
children.text: Anyone who, on or before the last day of the applicable transition period under paragraph (2), and without the consent of the rights owner, engages in covered activity with respect to a sound recording fixed before , shall be subject to the remedies provided in sections 502 through 505 and 1203 to the same extent as an infringer of copyright or a person that engages in unauthorized activity under chapter 12.February 15, 1972

children.label: I
children.text: through December 31 of the year that is 95 years after the year of first publication; and

children.label: II
children.text: for a further transition period as prescribed under subparagraph (B) of this paragraph; and

children.label: ii
children.text: shall not apply to any sound recording after .February 15, 2067

children.heading: .—Pre-1923 recordings
children.label: i .—Pre-1923 recordings
children.text: In the case of a sound recording first published before , the transition period described in subparagraph (A)(i)(II) shall end on December 31 of the year that is 3 years after the date of enactment of this section.January 1, 1923

children.heading: 1923–1946 .—recordings
children.label: ii 1923–1946 .—recordings
children.text: In the case of a sound recording first published during the period beginning on , and ending on , the transition period described in subparagraph (A)(i)(II) shall end on the date that is 5 years after the last day of the period described in subparagraph (A)(i)(I).January 1, 1923December 31, 1946

children.heading: 1947–1956 .—recordings
children.label: iii 1947–1956 .—recordings
children.text: In the case of a sound recording first published during the period beginning on , and ending on , the transition period described in subparagraph (A)(i)(II) shall end on the date that is 15 years after the last day of the period described in subparagraph (A)(i)(I).January 1, 1947December 31, 1956

children.heading: .—Post-1956 recordings
children.label: iv .—Post-1956 recordings
children.text: In the case of a sound recording fixed before , that is not described in clause (i), (ii), or (iii), the transition period described in subparagraph (A)(i)(II) shall end on .February 15, 1972February 15, 2067

children.heading: .—Rule of construction
children.label: 3 .—Rule of construction
children.text: For the purposes of this subsection, the term “anyone” includes any State, any instrumentality of a State, and any officer or employee of a State or instrumentality of a State acting in the official capacity of the officer or employee, as applicable.

the transmission or reproduction would satisfy the requirements for statutory licensing under section 112(e)(1) or section 114(d)(2), or would be exempt under section 114(d)(1), as the case may be, if the sound recording were fixed on or after ; andFebruary 15, 1972

the transmitting entity pays the statutory royalty for the transmission or reproduction pursuant to the rates and terms adopted under sections 112(e) and 114(f), and complies with other obligations, in the same manner as required by regulations adopted by the Copyright Royalty Judges under sections 112(e) and 114(f) for sound recordings that are fixed on or after , except in the case of a transmission that would be exempt under section 114(d)(1).February 15, 1972

children.heading: Certain Noncommercial Uses of Sound Recordings That Are Not Being Commercially Exploited.—
children.label: c Certain Noncommercial Uses of Sound Recordings That Are Not Being Commercially Exploited.—
i
children.text: in the records of schedules filed in the Copyright Office as described in subsection (f)(5)(A); and

children.label: ii
children.text: on services offering a comprehensive set of sound recordings for sale or streaming;

children.label: B
children.text: the person engaging in the noncommercial use files a notice identifying the sound recording and the nature of the use in the Copyright Office in accordance with the regulations issued under paragraph (3)(B); and

children.label: C
children.text: during the 90-day period beginning on the date on which the notice described in subparagraph (B) is indexed into the public records of the Copyright Office, the rights owner of the sound recording does not, in its discretion, opt out of the noncommercial use by filing notice thereof in the Copyright Office in accordance with the regulations issued under paragraph (5).

children.label: A
children.text: merely recovering costs of production and distribution of a sound recording resulting from a use otherwise permitted under this subsection does not itself necessarily constitute a commercial use of the sound recording;

children.label: B
children.text: the fact that a person engaging in the use of a sound recording also engages in commercial activities does not itself necessarily render the use commercial; and

children.label: C
children.text: the fact that a person files notice of a noncommercial use of a sound recording in accordance with the regulations issued under paragraph (3)(B) does not itself affect any limitation on the exclusive rights of a copyright owner described in section 107, 108, 109, 110, or 112(f) as applied to a claim under subsection (a) of this section pursuant to subsection (f)(1)(A) of this section.

children.label: A
children.text: provide specific, reasonable steps that, if taken by a filer, are sufficient to constitute a good faith, reasonable search under paragraph (1)(A) to determine whether a recording is being commercially exploited, including the services that satisfy the good faith, reasonable search requirement under paragraph (1)(A) for purposes of the safe harbor described in paragraph (4)(A); and

children.label: B
children.text: establish the form, content, and procedures for the filing of notices under paragraph (1)(B).

children.heading: .—In general
children.label: A .—In general
children.text: A person engaging in a noncommercial use of a sound recording otherwise permitted under this subsection who establishes that the person made a good faith, reasonable search under paragraph (1)(A) without finding commercial exploitation of the sound recording by or under the authority of the rights owner shall not be found to be in violation of subsection (a).

children.heading: .—Steps sufficient but not necessary
children.label: B .—Steps sufficient but not necessary
children.text: Taking the specific, reasonable steps identified by the Register of Copyrights in the regulations issued under paragraph (3)(A) shall be sufficient, but not necessary, for a filer to satisfy the requirement to conduct a good faith, reasonable search under paragraph (1)(A) for purposes of subparagraph (A) of this paragraph.

children.heading: .—In general
children.label: A .—In general
children.text: Not later than 180 days after the date of enactment of this section, the Register of Copyrights shall issue regulations establishing the form, content, and procedures for the rights owner of a sound recording that is the subject of a notice under paragraph (1)(B) to, in its discretion, file notice opting out of the covered activity described in the notice under paragraph (1)(B) during the 90-day period beginning on the date on which the notice under paragraph (1)(B) is indexed into the public records of the Copyright Office.

children.heading: .—Rule of construction
children.label: B .—Rule of construction
children.text: The fact that a rights holder opts out of a noncommercial use of a sound recording by filing notice thereof in the Copyright Office in accordance with the regulations issued under subparagraph (A) does not itself enlarge or diminish any limitation on the exclusive rights of a copyright owner described in section 107, 108, 109, 110, or 112(f) as applied to a claim under subsection (a) of this section pursuant to subsection (f)(1)(A) of this section.

children.heading: .—Filing of notices of noncommercial use
children.label: A .—Filing of notices of noncommercial use
children.text: Any person who willfully engages in a pattern or practice of filing a notice of noncommercial use of a sound recording as described in paragraph (1)(B) fraudulently describing the use proposed, or knowing that the use proposed is not permitted under this subsection, shall be assessed a civil penalty in an amount that is not less than $250, and not more than $1000, for each such notice, in addition to any other remedies that may be available under this title based on the actual use made.

children.heading: .—In general
children.label: i .—In general
children.text: Any person who files an opt-out notice as described in paragraph (1)(C), knowing that the person is not the rights owner or authorized to act on behalf of the rights owner of the sound recording to which the notice pertains, shall be assessed a civil penalty in an amount not less than $250, and not more than $1,000, for each such notice.

children.heading: .—Pattern or practice
children.label: ii .—Pattern or practice
children.text: Any person who engages in a pattern or practice of making filings as described in clause (i) shall be assessed a civil penalty in an amount not less than $10,000 for each such filing.

children.label: i
children.text: does not require specific intent to defraud; and

children.label: I
children.text: has actual knowledge of the information;

children.label: II
children.text: acts in deliberate ignorance of the truth or falsity of the information; or

children.label: III
children.text: acts in grossly negligent disregard of the truth or falsity of the information.

children.heading: Payment of Royalties for Transmissions of Performances by Direct Licensing of Statutory Services.—
children.label: d Payment of Royalties for Transmissions of Performances by Direct Licensing of Statutory Services.—

children.heading: .—In general
children.label: 1 .—In general
children.text: A public performance by means of a digital audio transmission of a sound recording fixed before , shall, for purposes of subsection (a), be considered to be authorized and made with the consent of the rights owner if the transmission is made pursuant to a license agreement voluntarily negotiated at any time between the rights owner and the entity performing the sound recording.February 15, 1972

children.heading: Payment of royalties to nonprofit collective under certain license agreements.—
children.label: 2 Payment of royalties to nonprofit collective under certain license agreements.—

children.heading: .—Licenses entered into on or after date of enactment
children.label: A .—Licenses entered into on or after date of enactment
i
children.text: the licensee shall, with respect to such transmission, pay to the collective designated to distribute receipts from the licensing of transmissions in accordance with section 114(f), 50 percent of the performance royalties for that transmission due under the license; and

children.label: ii
children.text: the royalties paid under clause (i) shall be fully credited as payments due under the license.

children.heading: .—Certain agreements entered into before enactment
children.label: B .—Certain agreements entered into before enactment
aa
children.text: the rights owner’s total gross performance royalty fee receipts or settlement monies received for all such transmissions covered under the license or settlement agreement, as applicable; and

children.label: bb
children.text: the rights owner’s total payments for outside legal expenses, including any payments of third-party claims, that are directly attributable to the license or settlement agreement, as applicable; and

children.label: II
children.text: the amount of any royalty receipts or settlement monies under the agreement that are distributed by the rights owner to featured and nonfeatured artists before the date of enactment of this section; and

children.label: ii
children.text: the royalties paid under clause (i) shall be fully credited as payments due under the license or settlement agreement, as applicable.

children.heading: .—Distribution of royalties and settlement monies by collective
children.label: 3 .—Distribution of royalties and settlement monies by collective
children.text: The collective described in paragraph (2) shall, in accordance with subparagraphs (B) through (D) of section 114(g)(2), and paragraphs (5) and (6) of section 114(g), distribute the royalties or settlement monies received under paragraph (2) under a license or settlement described in paragraph (2), which shall be the only payments to which featured and nonfeatured artists are entitled by virtue of the transmissions described in paragraph (2), except for settlement monies described in paragraph (2) that are distributed by the rights owner to featured and nonfeatured artists before the date of enactment of this section.

children.heading: Payment of royalties under license agreements entered before enactment or not otherwise described in paragraph (2).—
children.label: 4 Payment of royalties under license agreements entered before enactment or not otherwise described in paragraph (2).—

children.heading: .—In general
children.label: A .—In general
children.text: To the extent that a license agreement described in paragraph (1) entered into before the date of enactment of this section, or any other license agreement not as described in paragraph (2), extends to a public performance by means of a digital audio transmission of a sound recording fixed before , that meets the conditions of subsection (b), the payments made by the licensee pursuant to the license shall be made in accordance with the agreement.February 15, 1972

children.heading: .—Additional payments not required
children.label: B .—Additional payments not required
children.text: To the extent that a licensee has made, or will make in the future, payments pursuant to a license as described in subparagraph (A), the provisions of paragraphs (2) and (3) shall not require any additional payments from, or additional financial obligations on the part of, the licensee.

children.heading: .—Rule of construction
children.label: C .—Rule of construction
children.text: Nothing in this subsection may be construed to prohibit the collective designated to distribute receipts from the licensing of transmissions in accordance with section 114(f) from administering royalty payments under any license not described in paragraph (2).

children.heading: Preemption With Respect to Certain Past Acts.—
children.label: e Preemption With Respect to Certain Past Acts.—
A
children.text: the digital audio transmission would have satisfied the requirements for statutory licensing under section 114(d)(2) or been exempt under section 114(d)(1), or the reproduction would have satisfied the requirements of section 112(e)(1), as the case may be, if the sound recording were fixed on or after ; andFebruary 15, 1972

children.label: i
children.text: except in the case of a transmission that would have been exempt under section 114(d)(1), not later than 270 days after the date of enactment of this section, the transmitting entity pays statutory royalties and provides notice of the use of the relevant sound recordings in the same manner as required by regulations adopted by the Copyright Royalty Judges for sound recordings that are fixed on or after , for all the digital audio transmissions and reproductions satisfying the requirements for statutory licensing under sections 112(e)(1) and 114(d)(2) during the 3 years before that date of enactment; orFebruary 15, 1972

children.label: ii
children.text: an agreement voluntarily negotiated between the rights owner and the entity performing the sound recording (including a litigation settlement agreement entered into before the date of enactment of this section) authorizes or waives liability for any such transmission or reproduction and the transmitting entity has paid for and reported such digital audio transmission under that agreement.

children.heading: .—Rule of construction for common law copyright
children.label: 2 .—Rule of construction for common law copyright
children.text: For purposes of paragraph (1), a claim of common law copyright or equivalent right under the laws of any State includes a claim that characterizes conduct subject to that paragraph as an unlawful distribution, act of record piracy, or similar violation.

children.heading: .—Rule of construction for public performance rights
children.label: 3 .—Rule of construction for public performance rights
children.text: Nothing in this section may be construed to recognize or negate the existence of public performance rights in sound recordings under the laws of any State.

children.heading: Fair use; uses by libraries, archives, and educational institutions.—
children.label: 1 Fair use; uses by libraries, archives, and educational institutions.—

children.heading: .—In general
children.label: A .—In general
children.text: The limitations on the exclusive rights of a copyright owner described in sections 107, 108, 109, 110, and 112(f) shall apply to a claim under subsection (a) with respect to a sound recording fixed before .February 15, 1972

children.heading: .—Rule of construction for section 108(h)
children.label: B .—Rule of construction for section 108(h)
children.text: With respect to the application of section 108(h) to a claim under subsection (a) with respect to a sound recording fixed before , the phrase “during the last 20 years of any term of copyright of a published work” in such section 108(h) shall be construed to mean at any time after the date of enactment of this section.February 15, 1972

children.heading: .—Actions
children.label: 2 .—Actions
children.text: The limitations on actions described in section 507 shall apply to a claim under subsection (a) with respect to a sound recording fixed before .February 15, 1972

children.heading: .—Material online
children.label: 3 .—Material online
children.text: Section 512 shall apply to a claim under subsection (a) with respect to a sound recording fixed before .February 15, 1972

children.heading: .—Principles of equity
children.label: 4 .—Principles of equity
children.text: Principles of equity apply to remedies for a violation of this section to the same extent as such principles apply to remedies for infringement of copyright.

children.heading: Filing requirement for statutory damages and attorneys’ fees.—
children.label: 5 Filing requirement for statutory damages and attorneys’ fees.—

children.heading: Filing of information on sound recordings.—
children.label: A Filing of information on sound recordings.—
I
children.text: the rights owner has filed with the Copyright Office a schedule that specifies the title, artist, and rights owner of the sound recording and contains such other information, as practicable, as the Register of Copyrights prescribes by regulation; and

children.label: II
children.text: the use occurs after the end of the 90-day period beginning on the date on which the information described in subclause (I) is indexed into the public records of the Copyright Office.

children.label: I
children.text: establish the form, content, and procedures for the filing of schedules under clause (i);

children.label: II
children.text: provide that a person may request that the person receive timely notification of a filing described in subclause (I); and

children.label: III
children.text: set forth the manner in which a person may make a request under subclause (II).

children.heading: Filing of contact information for transmitting entities.—
children.label: B Filing of contact information for transmitting entities.—

children.heading: .—Filing requirement
children.label: i .—Filing requirement
children.text: Not later than 30 days after the date of enactment of this section, the Register of Copyrights shall issue regulations establishing the form, content, and procedures for the filing of contact information by any entity that, as of the date of enactment of this section, performs a sound recording fixed before , by means of a digital audio transmission.February 15, 1972

children.heading: .—Time limit on filings
children.label: ii .—Time limit on filings
children.text: The Register of Copyrights may accept filings under clause (i) only until the 180th day after the date of enactment of this section.

children.heading: Limitation on statutory damages and attorneys’ fees.—
children.label: iii Limitation on statutory damages and attorneys’ fees.—
aa
children.text: is sent by or on behalf of the rights owner of the sound recording;

children.label: bb
children.text: states that the entity is not legally authorized to use that sound recording under subsection (a); and

children.label: cc
children.text: identifies the sound recording in a schedule conforming to the requirements prescribed by the regulations issued under subparagraph (A)(ii).

children.heading: .—Undeliverable notices
children.label: II .—Undeliverable notices
children.text: In any case in which a notice under subclause (I) is sent to an entity by mail or courier service and the notice is returned to the sender because the entity either is no longer located at the address provided in the contact information filed under clause (i) or has refused to accept delivery, or the notice is sent by electronic mail and is undeliverable, the 90-day period under subclause (I) shall begin on the date of the attempted delivery.

children.heading: .—Section 412
children.label: C .—Section 412
children.text: Section 412 shall not limit an award of statutory damages under section 504(c) or attorneys’ fees under section 505 with respect to a covered activity in violation of subsection (a).

children.heading: .—In general
children.label: A .—In general
children.text: Subject to subparagraph (B), no provision of this title shall apply to or limit the remedies available under this section except as otherwise provided in this section.

children.heading: .—Applicability of definitions
children.label: B .—Applicability of definitions
children.text: Any term used in this section that is defined in section 101 shall have the meaning given that term in section 101.

children.heading: 230 .—Application of SectionSafe Harbor
children.label: g 230 .—Application of SectionSafe Harbor
children.text: For purposes of section 230 of the Communications Act of 1934 (), subsection (a) shall be considered to be a “law pertaining to intellectual property” under subsection (e)(2) of such section 230.47 U.S.C. 230

children.label: A
children.text: subsections (d) and (e) of section 201 and section 204 shall apply to a transfer described in subsection ()(2)(B) to the same extent as with respect to a transfer of copyright ownership; andl

children.label: B
children.text: notwithstanding section 411, that rights owner may institute an action with respect to a violation of this section to the same extent as the owner of an exclusive right under a copyright may institute an action under section 501(b).

children.label: A
children.text: Section 112(e)(2).

children.label: B
children.text: Section 112(e)(7).

children.label: C
children.text: Section 114(e).

children.label: D
children.text: Section 114(h).

children.heading: .—Ephemeral Recordings
children.label: i .—Ephemeral Recordings
children.text: An authorized reproduction made under this section shall be subject to section 112(g) to the same extent as a reproduction of a sound recording fixed on or after .February 15, 1972

children.heading: .—Rule of Construction
children.label: j .—Rule of Construction
children.text: A rights owner of, or featured recording artist who performs on, a sound recording under this chapter shall be deemed to be an interested copyright party, as defined in section 1001, to the same extent as a copyright owner or featured recording artist under chapter 10.

children.heading: .—Treatment of States and State Instrumentalities, Officers, and Employees
children.label: k .—Treatment of States and State Instrumentalities, Officers, and Employees
children.text: Any State, and any instrumentality, officer, or employee described in subsection (a)(3), shall be subject to the provisions of this section in the same manner and to the same extent as any nongovernmental entity.

children.heading: .—Covered activity
children.label: 1 .—Covered activity
children.text: The term “covered activity” means any activity that the copyright owner of a sound recording would have the exclusive right to do or authorize under section 106 or 602, or that would violate section 1201 or 1202, if the sound recording were fixed on or after .February 15, 1972

children.label: A
children.text: the person that has the exclusive right to reproduce a sound recording under the laws of any State, as of the day before the date of enactment of this section; or

children.label: i
children.text: subsections (d) and (e) of section 201; and

children.label: ii
children.text: section 204.
